status.boundary_rule: 5 MHz remains functional simulation only until bench or source-backed physical timing evidence is added.

clock_profiles.name: manual_push_switch_100_ticks
clock_profiles.pass_condition: Exactly one clean logical clock edge per push; no double-trigger or missed tick.

physical_test_sweep.claim_boundary: Completing source-backed definitions does not prove physical timing; every voltage/clock profile needs bench evidence.

part_timing_sources.physical_caveat: The 70 ns model read delay is not a selected physical speed grade.
DB source is datasheet-backed; selected physical SRAM marking and measured access/output-disable timing remain required.
DB source is datasheet-backed; selected physical SRAM marking and measured access/output-disable timing remain required.

physical_timing_assumptions.requirement: Choose and record the actual EEPROM speed grade before any physical 5 MHz claim.
Add selected physical SRAM read and output-float evidence before tightening RAM turnaround claims.
Circuit paths that clock 74HC574 inputs must leave this setup/hold window at the physical pins.
Program-counter load/count controls must settle before the clock using the largest relevant setup value.

physical_timing_assumptions.required_deadband: Disable the old DBUS driver and wait for its output-float time before enabling the next DBUS driver.
physical_timing_assumptions.requirement: No bus turnaround path may be described as physically proven until the deadband is quantified.

physical_candidate_paths.claim_boundary: This is a selected-part timing candidate only; breadboard wiring delay, fanout, edge quality, and measured signal integrity are not included.
A slower EEPROM speed grade fails this 5 MHz source-backed ROM fetch budget before wiring margin is added.
RAM read/write turnaround has source-backed chip rows, but cannot be physically signed off until the installed SRAM marking and measured bus timing are recorded.
The old DBUS driver must be disabled and floated before U7 or RAM is allowed to drive the same bus.

physical_bench_evidence_required.pass_condition: Clock and reset edges are monotonic at TTL inputs, with no double-triggering and no reset release runt pulse.
Exactly one of T0/T1/T2 is high after each clean clock edge across at least 100 observed ticks per voltage/clock profile.
No overlap window shows two enabled DBUS output drivers; measured deadband is greater than selected memory output-float plus buffer enable skew.

required_evidence: selected voltage test row: 4.5 V, 5.0 V, 5.5 V
physical_bench_evidence_required.pass_condition: The physical chips installed match the speed-grade rows used in timing calculations.

required_measurements.how_to_capture: Photograph or transcribe the part top markings for the EEPROM and SRAM installed in the build.
required_measurements.pass_condition: The installed EEPROM and SRAM markings match datasheet timing rows used by this file.
required_measurements.student_note: A chip with the same logic function can still be too slow if its speed grade is different.

required_measurements.how_to_capture: Scope ROM /OE, RAM /OE or /CE, U7 /OE, WR_DIR, and one DBUS bit during a read-to-write and write-to-read transition.
required_measurements.pass_condition: The old driver is off before the new driver turns on, with measured deadband greater than selected memory output-float plus buffer enable skew.
required_measurements.student_note: A bus can look correct after it settles while still fighting for a short time during the change.

required_measurements.how_to_capture: Scope CLK with T0/T1/T2 and the memory or U7 enable signals across at least 100 ticks.
required_measurements.pass_condition: T0/T1/T2 remain one-hot and every enable transition has a named clock-phase gap before the next possible bus driver.
required_measurements.student_note: The phase LEDs show which step is active; the scope proves the edges are not overlapping too fast to see.

required_measurements.how_to_capture: Scope VCC at the breadboard rails and at a far IC while toggling reset, manual clock, 1 MHz, 2 MHz, and 5 MHz if attempted.
required_measurements.pass_condition: VCC stays inside the selected 74HC datasheet range and clock/reset edges are monotonic at TTL inputs.
required_measurements.student_note: Good logic equations still fail if the power rail bounces or the clock edge wiggles.

required_measurements.how_to_capture: Scope a fast control edge at the driver pin and at the destination input on representative short, medium, long, and bus nets at 4.5 V, 5.0 V, and 5.5 V.
required_measurements.pass_condition: Measured destination rise/fall and threshold-crossing delay are recorded before any RC estimate is used for signoff.
required_measurements.student_note: Breadboard wires behave a little like hidden resistors and capacitors; simulation can estimate this, but the scope tells us the real value.

board_parasitic_model.why_not_chip_definition: Chip definitions describe datasheet behavior at the IC pins. Breadboard capacitance, wire resistance, contact resistance, probe loading, and layout-dependent crosstalk change with the build and must stay in circuit/physical timing artifacts.
board_parasitic_model.threshold_delay_rule: Use measured threshold-crossing delay when available. Before measurement, treat 2.2*tau as a conservative 10%-to-90% settling estimate, not proof.

source_resistance_ohm.notes: Use datasheet output resistance or measure with the installed chip/load.

wire_capacitance_pf.notes: Depends on breadboard length, adjacent wires, and probes.

chip_input_capacitance_pf.notes: Sum every destination input on the net.

probe_capacitance_pf.notes: A scope probe can add enough capacitance to change an edge.

pullup_pulldown_resistance_ohm.notes: Manual clock/reset and any RC debounce path must include this.

representative_nets_to_calibrate.why: A slow or ringing clock can double-trigger edge-sensitive chips.
Reset release timing controls first known state.
Shared bus capacitance affects read/write turnaround and threshold timing.
Instruction/data capture depends on stable bus before latch edge.
Output-enable and write timing determine bus fight deadband.

board_parasitic_model.claim_boundary: RC simulation may guide wiring and margin decisions, but physical pass/fail requires scoped edge quality and threshold timing at the real destination pins.

timing_coverage_matrix.claim_boundary: functional timing plus model slack
functional timing plus model slack
functional select timing plus model slack
bus logic proven; physical overlap timing still requires scope/source evidence
functional timing plus model slack; selected ROM grade required
functional store timing plus model slack; selected SRAM/ROM output-disable timing required
functional timing plus source-backed SRAM options; installed SRAM marking and measured read/float timing required
functional edge behavior only; external switch edge quality must be measured
functional read timing plus model slack; ROM output-float evidence required
tightest listed model path; physical 5 MHz still not proven
functional timing plus model slack
functional timing plus model slack
virtual observation helpers do not prove hardware timing
control equations proven functionally; gate delay timing remains covered by specific paths
functional reset/clock timing proven; physical edge quality requires scope evidence
functional trace timing; physical memory/clock evidence covered by lower-level paths
functional trace timing; physical store/load deadband covered by lower-level paths
functional trace timing; page-register setup covered by lower-level paths
functional trace timing; external IRQ edge quality requires measurement
functional boot trace only; physical single-step and edge evidence still required
functional Lab 13 marker trace only; physical single-step, oscillator, and bus-deadband evidence still required
virtual whole-system chip-level stress only; physical voltage/frequency/scope evidence still required

system_timing_gates.name: physical_timing_gate
system_timing_gates.command: scope and bench evidence, not software-only

system_hazard_gates.software_gate: Recompute slack for every propagation path against 50 kHz, 1 MHz, 2 MHz, and 5 MHz profiles.
system_hazard_gates.hardware_gate: Measure selected-chip timing assumptions before accepting any high-speed result.

system_hazard_gates.software_gate: Assert one active IBUS driver and one active DBUS driver for every phase/vector; force unsafe conflicts to prove detection.
system_hazard_gates.hardware_gate: Scope output-enable transitions and prove old drivers are disabled before new drivers turn on.

system_hazard_gates.software_gate: Check positive-edge captures, negative/active-low controls, no-edge holds, and reset priority for every clocked circuit.
system_hazard_gates.hardware_gate: Single-step and scope the real edge that changes each register; confirm opposite edge/no edge holds.

system_hazard_gates.software_gate: Track named paths from source outputs through chips to destination setup windows and verify slack arithmetic.
system_hazard_gates.hardware_gate: Measure critical path settle time at destination inputs before the active clock edge.
blocks_system_signoff_if: critical path not settled before latch edge

setup_hold_requirements.note: Students should read this as: data must already be steady before the clock edge and must not change immediately after it.
The ENP/ENT setup is the largest listed 74HC161 requirement and is the conservative counter-control value.
Ordinary RV8GR stores target RAM; ROM-page stores are documented as bus-safe but not as normal writable memory.

propagation_paths.to: U8 serial inputs for next rising edge
notes: Feedback must not be treated as changing the phase that was just clocked.

propagation_paths.to: next counter enable before rising clock
notes: Uses the largest listed 74HC161 control setup value.
Physical 5 MHz still needs bench wiring and fanout evidence.
The path must be settled before the PC clock edge that samples /PC_LD.

through: 74HC04 A15 complement when RAM is selected
propagation_paths.to: ABUS plus complementary ROM/RAM chip selects
notes: SRC/STR must be stable before T2 data-address use.

propagation_paths.to: U5/U6 74HC574 data inputs before T0/T1 capture
notes: AT28C256 speed grade can be 150 ns or slower in the datasheet table, so the 70 ns model read is not enough to prove physical 5 MHz.

propagation_paths.from: ABUS, ROM/RAM select, and memory output
notes: RAM and ROM selects must remain mutually exclusive across the A15 boundary.
IBUS must satisfy the U32 setup/hold window around DP_Load rising.
IBUS must satisfy the U23 setup/hold window around PG_CLK rising.

propagation_paths.to: RAM data pins before /WE write window
notes: Store controls and data path are both T2-sensitive; physical proof must check that /WE is not asserted before data is valid.

propagation_paths.to: U9 74HC574 AC data inputs before ACC_CLK
notes: This is the tightest listed model path; it is still not a physical 5 MHz proof.

propagation_paths.to: Z_flag valid for later branch decision
notes: Branch control must not sample Z before this path settles.

bus_race_notes.risk: ROM output disable and U7 write enable can overlap during read-to-store direction changes.
bus_race_notes.current_evidence: Functional bus-ownership tests catch forced simultaneous drivers.
bus_race_notes.still_needed: Physical deadband or source-backed enable/disable timing for breadboard wiring; AT28C256 output-float values are speed-grade dependent.

bus_race_notes.risk: RAM output and U7 write driver must not both drive DBUS during T2 store.
bus_race_notes.current_evidence: Store path ties RAM /WE LOW during stores and tests forced DBUS conflict states.
bus_race_notes.still_needed: RAM output-disable timing and /WE edge timing from the selected physical SRAM datasheet.

bus_race_notes.risk: U14, U34, and U7 are all possible IBUS drivers and must remain one-hot.
bus_race_notes.current_evidence: BusOwnership and AluAccumulator artifacts name unsafe multi-driver states.
bus_race_notes.still_needed: Future tests should consume this artifact and assert no profile claims 5 MHz hardware safety without timing evidence.

student_summary: 50 kHz, 1 MHz, and 2 MHz have large positive model-delay margins for the listed paths.

5 MHz has a 200 ns period and positive model slack on these paths, but it is still functional-only because breadboard wiring, output-disable races, and selected memory speed grades are not proven here.
Setup means data is ready before a clock edge; hold means it stays steady just after the edge; bus race means two chips might briefly try to drive the same wire.
